Identification.LongTitle: An Act to give effect to the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty and to make consequential amendments to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act
Identification.RunningHead: Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act

Provision.Text: This enactment gives effect to the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty and makes consequential amendments to other Acts.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to give effect to the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty and to make consequential amendments to other Acts

TableOfProvisions.Label: â

Text.XRefExternal: Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act

TableOfProvisions.Text: Judicial notice â Treaty and tax treatment agreement
Judicial notice â Red River MÃ©tis law

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to achieving reconciliation with the Red River MÃ©tis through renewed government-to-government relationships based on the recognition of rights, respect, cooperation and partnership;
Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that the inherent right of self-government is a right recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982;

Whereas the treaty entitled Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty was signed on November 30, 2024 on behalf of the Red River MÃ©tis and His Majesty in right of Canada;

Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples and the Red River MÃ©tis and the Government of Canada view the Treaty as contributing to the implementation of that Declaration;
And whereas ratification of the Treaty requires the coming into force of an Act of Parliament that gives effect to it;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: federal lawâhas the meaning assigned by the definition Federal Law in paragraph 1 of the Treaty.â(rÃ¨gle de droit fÃ©dÃ©rale)
Manitoba MÃ©tis Federationâhas the meaning assigned by the definition MMF in paragraph 1 of the Treaty.â(FÃ©dÃ©ration MÃ©tisse du Manitoba)
Red River MÃ©tisâhas the same meaning as in paragraph 1 of the Treaty.â(MÃ©tis de la RiviÃ¨re Rouge)
Red River MÃ©tis lawâhas the meaning assigned by the definition Red River MÃ©tis Law in paragraph 1 of the Treaty.â(loi des MÃ©tis de la RiviÃ¨re Rouge)
tax treatment agreementâhas the meaning assigned by the definition Tax Treatment Agreement in paragraph 1 of the Treaty.â(accord sur le traitement fiscal)

Treatyâmeans the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty, signed on behalf of the Red River MÃ©tis and His Majesty in right of Canada on November 30, 2024, as amended from time to time in accordance with its provisions.â(traitÃ©)

Section.Text: For greater certainty, the Treaty is a treaty within the meaning of sections 25 and 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Subsection.Text: The Treaty is approved, given effect and declared valid and has the force of law.

For greater certainty, a person or body has the powers, rights, privileges and benefits conferred on the person or body by the Treaty and must perform the duties, and is subject to the liabilities, imposed on the person or body by the Treaty.
For greater certainty, the Treaty is binding on, and may be relied on by, all persons and bodies.
In the event of an inconsistency or conflict between the Treaty and this Act or any other federal law, the Treaty prevails to the extent of the inconsistency or conflict.
In the event of an inconsistency or conflict between this Act or any other federal law, this Act prevails to the extent of the inconsistency or conflict.

Section.Text: The tax treatment agreement is approved, given effect and declared valid and has the force of law during the period that it is in effect.
The tax treatment agreement does not form part of the Treaty, and it is not a treaty or a land claims agreement within the meaning of sections 25 and 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.MarginalNote: Judicial notice â Treaty and tax treatment agreement

Subsection.Text: Judicial notice must be taken of the Treaty and the tax treatment agreement.
The Treaty and the tax treatment agreement must be published by the Kingâs Printer.

A copy of the Treaty or the tax treatment agreement published by the Kingâs Printer is evidence of the Treaty or the tax treatment agreement, and of its contents, and a copy purporting to be published by the Kingâs Printer is presumed to be so published unless the contrary is shown.

Section.MarginalNote: Judicial notice â Red River MÃ©tis law

Subsection.Text: Judicial notice must be taken of Red River MÃ©tis laws.
A copy of a Red River MÃ©tis law purporting to be deposited in the public registry referred to in paragraph 57 of the Treaty is evidence of that law and of its contents unless the contrary is shown.

Section.Text: The Federal Court may undertake judicial review of an administrative decision taken by the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation in the exercise of a statutory power of decision under a Red River MÃ©tis law, provided that all procedures for appeal or review applicable to that decision and set out in a Red River MÃ©tis law have been exhausted.
Neither Red River MÃ©tis laws nor any instruments made under the Treaty are statutory instruments within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make any orders and regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary for the purpose of carrying out any of the provisions of the Treaty, this Act, the tax treatment agreement or any other agreement related to the implementation of the Treaty.

The Governor in Council must ensure that the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation has a meaningful opportunity to collaborate in the policy development leading to the making of the regulations under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Any action taken or decision made by the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation before the day on which the treaty came into force is deemed to have been validly taken or made under the Treaty to the extent that the action taken or decision made would have been valid under the Treaty.
Subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h.1):

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation, as defined in section 2 of the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act;

Subsection.Text: The expression aboriginal government in paragraph (1)(e) means
Paragraph 8(2)(f) of the Privacy Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (vii.1):

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation, as defined in section 2 of the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act;

Subsection.Text: Subject to any other Act of Parliament, personal information under the control of a government institution may be disclosed

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of administering or enforcing any law or carrying out a lawful investigation, under an agreement or arrangement between the Government of Canada or any of its institutions and any of the following entities or any of their institutions:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8(7) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h.1):

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation, as defined in section 2 of the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act;

Subsection.Text: The expression aboriginal government in paragraph (2)(k) means

Section.Text: Subsection 19(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e.1):

Paragraph.Label: (e.2)
Paragraph.Text: the Manitoba MÃ©tis Federation, as defined in section 2 of the Red River MÃ©tis Self-Government Recognition and Implementation Treaty Act;

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the head of a government institution shall refuse to disclose any personal information requested under subsection 12(1) that was obtained in confidence from

Section.Text: If Bill C-10, introduced in the 1st session of the 45th Parliament and entitled the Commissioner for Modern Treaty Implementation Act, receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both that Act and section 4 of this Act are in force, the schedule to that Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:
